mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL > 250) triggers aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust economic demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 50% of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE < 4.0%) signals tight labor markets that push wage inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 40% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) indicate weakening supply chains, reducing corporate profitability and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines for two months.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with rising housing permits (HOUST) creates inflationary pressures that force rate hikes, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint increase in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce negative 20d returns in > 50% of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals a resilient labor market that boosts consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in < 30% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.0%) during a high-inflation environment (CPIAUCSL > 250) triggers recession fears, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint rise in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to produce negative 20d returns in > 50% of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.5.
